title: Postmortem - Incident #7401: TLS Certificate Expiration Outage
content:
Postmortem - Incident #7401: TLS Certificate Expiration Outage

### Incident Summary
On May 24, all user HTTPS requests to api-gateway failed. Browsers displayed security warning SSL certificate expired. Latency rose to infinity.

### Root Cause
The automated ACME renewal cron job was silent because its endpoint was blocked by a firewall configuration change. The cert expired after 90 days.

### Resolution
SRE updated the firewall rules to allow certbot outbound connection. Run certbot manually to renew the certificate and reload the Nginx gateway configs.

### Action Items
- Add external uptime monitoring of SSL cert expiration dates.
- Configure slack/email notifications 14 days before certificate expiration.
